title: Curations.DEV suggestion plan
description: The public contract for curator-generated suggestion plans (schema 0.1.0). A plan is a recorded artifact, not a chat message: it names the run-record it responds to, cites every source that informed it, and emits steps the OWNER executes with their own entitlements. The platform never writes to an end-user repository. A re-run that acted on a plan carries this plan's plan_id in its run-record suggestion_plan_ref field — that link is what turns deltas into intervention->outcome rows.

plan_id.description: Unique id for this plan; referenced by later run-records.

responds_to_run.description: run_id of the run-record whose findings this plan addresses.

prompt_components.description: Versioned metaprompt parts assembled for this plan (capability manifest, category snapshot, lineage digest, ...). Prompts are composed, versioned data — never an unversioned blob.

grounding.description: World-state provenance. Null when the plan used no live grounding.

citations.description: Every source that informed the plan. An uncited claim does not ship — plans with an empty citations list are invalid by policy for generative content and permitted only for purely deterministic template plans.

kind.description: Execution lane. All lanes run on the owner's side with the owner's entitlements.

body.description: The runnable content: a command, a prompt, a workflow snippet, or plain-language instructions.

addresses_check.description: check_id of the finding this step addresses, when applicable.

consent_note.description: Plain-language statement of what executing this plan shares back, if anything. Absence means: nothing is shared back automatically.
